column1: This file is a cleaned and updated candidate-ranking layer for the current version of Programmable Neurorepair.
It should not be read as a flat legacy ranking alone. It now reflects the current project reading:
The top current candidates are no longer only high-ranking genes. They are now best read as differentiated control-layer roles inside the broader state-transition architecture.
- external support reading: externally interpretable in Dataset B; not a simple terminal-best-state marker
- note: Best read as movement-opening logic rather than naive monotonic higher-is-better repair signal.
- external support reading: externally supported in Dataset B with WT > PLP_iCKO > Sox10_iCKO style ordering
- note: Best read as commitment-stabilizing and directionality-preserving logic.
- external support reading: externally supported in Dataset B with WT > PLP_iCKO > Sox10_iCKO style ordering
- note: Best read as consolidation, maturation, and durability support rather than movement-opening logic.
## How to read the remaining rows

The remaining rows are primarily supporting repair-signature or repair-state-anchor candidates. They remain useful, but they should not be confused with the core minimal architecture unless later evidence upgrades them into a control-role interpretation.
This table is a current structured prioritization artifact. It is not a final mechanistic proof and should not be treated as the full project on its own.
